fact: The first official cricket Test match was played in 1877 between Australia and England at the Melbourne Cricket Ground (MCG). Australia won by 45 runs.
The ICC Cricket World Cup was first held in 1975 in England. The West Indies won the inaugural tournament, defeating Australia in the final at Lord's.
India won the ICC Cricket World Cup in 2011 on home soil, defeating Sri Lanka in the final at the Wankhede Stadium in Mumbai. It was India's second World Cup title after 1983.
The Indian Premier League (IPL), founded in 2008, is a Twenty20 cricket league and one of the most-watched sports leagues in the world, featuring franchise teams representing Indian cities.
Sachin Tendulkar holds the record for the most international runs and centuries in both Test and One Day International (ODI) cricket, and is the only player to score 100 international centuries.
The FIFA World Cup was first held in 1930. Uruguay hosted and won the tournament, defeating Argentina 4-2 in the final match in Montevideo.
Brazil has won the FIFA World Cup a record five times: in 1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, and 2002.
Lionel Messi captained Argentina to victory at the 2022 FIFA World Cup in Qatar, defeating France on penalties after a 3-3 draw in the final.
The UEFA Champions League is Europe's premier annual club football competition, with Real Madrid holding the record for the most titles.

The offside rule in football states that a player is in an offside position if they are nearer to the opponent's goal line than both the ball and the second-last opponent when the ball is played to them.
The four Grand Slam tournaments in tennis are the Australian Open, the French Open (Roland Garros), Wimbledon, and the US Open.
Novak Djokovic holds the record for the most men's Grand Slam singles titles in the Open Era, surpassing Roger Federer and Rafael Nadal.
Serena Williams won 23 Grand Slam singles titles during the Open Era, the most by any player, male or female, in that period.
Wimbledon, first held in 1877, is the oldest tennis tournament in the world and is the only Grand Slam still played on grass courts.
A tennis match is scored in points (15, 30, 40, game), games, and sets. Most matches are best-of-three sets, while men's Grand Slam matches are best-of-five sets.
The Thomas Cup, established in 1948, is the premier international men's team badminton championship. India won its historic first title in 2022 by defeating Indonesia 3-0.
The Uber Cup is the women's team equivalent of the Thomas Cup, first held in 1956-57, and has historically been dominated by China, Japan, and Indonesia.
Badminton has been part of the Summer Olympics since the 1992 Barcelona Games, with China historically being the most successful nation in Olympic badminton.
PV Sindhu became the first Indian woman to win an Olympic silver medal in badminton at the 2016 Rio Olympics, and later won bronze at the 2020 Tokyo Olympics.
A standard badminton match is played to the best of three games, with each game played to 21 points, requiring a two-point lead to win.
The NBA (National Basketball Association), founded in 1946, is the premier professional basketball league in the United States and Canada.
The Boston Celtics and Los Angeles Lakers hold the record for the most NBA championship titles, both having won 17 championships each as of the 2024 season.
Michael Jordan won six NBA championships with the Chicago Bulls in the 1990s and is widely regarded as one of the greatest basketball players of all time.
Basketball was invented in 1891 by Dr. James Naismith, a Canadian physical education instructor, in Springfield, Massachusetts.
A standard NBA game consists of four quarters of 12 minutes each, while FIBA international games use four quarters of 10 minutes each.
